customer_addr:
34200 B Doctors Hammock Rd
Immokalee, FL 34142

citations.narrative:
At 7:49 am on November 26, 2025, two cynomolgus macaques were found deceased inside the observation room,
located on the North side of building A. The thermometer for that area showed an ambient temperature of 104°F. The
facility was experiencing unusually cold outside temperatures, and a heater was placed in the North side of the hallway,
near the observation room. The heater did not have an automatic shut-off, so the temperatures were being checked and
recorded regularly by trained employees. The training gave instructions on what to do if the temperature drops below a
certain level, however, no instructions were given on how to proceed if the temperature rises above a certain level.
Records show that the recorded temperature for the observation room was 104°F at 12:00 am, 98°F at 3:00 am, and
104°F at 6:00 am.
Failure to protect the nonhuman primates from temperature extremes can have a negative impact on their health and
well-being.
The ambient temperature in the sheltered part of the facility must not rise above 85°F (29.5 °C) for more than 4
consecutive hours when nonhuman primates are present. Corrective measures were already implemented by the time of
inspection.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with facility representatives.
Additional Inspectors:
Patricia Laracuente, VETERINARY MEDICAL OFFICER
Sarah Roloff n

narrative:
3.77(a)  Critical
   
Sheltered housing facilities. 
At 7:49 am on November 26, 2025, two cynomolgus macaques were found deceased inside the observation room, 
located on the North side of building A. The thermometer for that area showed an ambient temperature of 104°F. The 
facility was experiencing unusually cold outside temperatures, and a heater was placed in the North side of the hallway, 
near the observation room. The heater did not have an automatic shut-off, so the temperatures were being checked and 
recorded regularly by trained employees. The training gave instructions on what to do if the temperature drops below a 
certain level, however, no instructions were given on how to proceed if the temperature rises above a certain level. 
Records show that the recorded temperature for the observation room was 104°F at 12:00 am, 98°F at 3:00 am, and 
104°F at 6:00 am. 
 
Failure to protect the nonhuman primates from temperature extremes can have a negative impact on their health and 
well-being. 
 
The ambient temperature in the sheltered part of the facility must not rise above 85°F (29.5 °C) for more than 4 
consecutive hours when nonhuman primates are present. Corrective measures were already implemented by the time of 
inspection. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with facility representatives. 
 
Additional Inspectors: 
Patricia Laracuente, VETERINARY MEDICAL OFFICER 
Sarah Roloff n
